steps.title: Identify the contrast between belief and evidence
steps.content: Scholars believed silk spread in the second century CE, but new evidence places it in the Indus Civilization 1,000 years earlier, fundamentally overturning the accepted view.

steps.title: Choose the word expressing this reversal
steps.content: ”Contradicted” means the evidence has shown the original belief to be false. An 1,000-year chronological gap directly contradicts the scholars' understanding of when silk spread. The other choices don't capture this sense of disproof: “investigated” means studied, “misinterpreted” means misunderstood, and “anticipated” means expected. That matches choice B — B is correct.
